history.content:
<thought>
I see that the goal is located at column F, row 5 ("G"), which is two rows ahead and two columns to the right of my current position. I am currently facing north at row 7, column D. The cell directly in front of me is a wall (#), so moving forward is not possible. To reach the goal, I need to head east. I will turn right to face east and then move forward towards the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
Now facing east, I am at row 7, column D. The cell in front (row 7, column E) is a wall (#), so I cannot move forward. To reach the goal at row 5, column B, I need to go west and then north. Since I am facing east, I will turn left to face north.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The observation has reset to the original view. I am facing north, with a wall (#) directly ahead at row 6, column D, so moving forward isn't possible. The goal is at row 5, column F. To reach it, I need to move east. I will turn right to face east and move forward towards the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The observation has changed back to a view where I'm facing north at row 7, column D, with a wall (#) directly in front at row 6, column D; moving forward is not possible. The goal (G) is at row 5, column B, to my left and a bit ahead. To reach it safely, I will turn left to face west, then move forward in the next steps.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The observation has reverted to the original egocentric view where I am at the bottom-center facing north. The path ahead is blocked by walls and lava to the left and right. The goal is visible two cells to the right and up. Given the obstacles ahead, turning left or right will allow movement around the walls. Since turning left previously aligned me more towards the goal location, I will turn left to face west to advance towards the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
Now I am facing west with the agent at row 7, column D, and the cell in front is a wall (#), so I cannot move forward. To find a way around the wall, I will turn left to face south since turning right would have me face north with unknown terrain.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing south at row 7, column D. The cell ahead (row 8, column D) isn't visible but given the wall pattern below in row 6 and 7, it is likely blocked or a wall (#) or unknown. The cells to my left and right are empty floor cells. Since I cannot move forward due to unknown terrain, turning right to face west where there are empty floor spaces to explore makes sense.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing west again at row 7, column D. The cell in front of me is a wall (#), so moving forward is not possible. Turning left to face south to explore new territory is the best option.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing south at row 7, column D again. The cell in front is a wall (#), so moving forward is blocked. Turning left to face east to check for possible safe paths seems like the best option to move forward.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing east at row 7, column D. The cell in front of me (column E) is a wall (#), so I cannot move forward. However, the cells to the left and right appear to be floor cells. Turning left to face north will allow me to explore and potentially move toward the goal at row 5, column B.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing north at row 7, column D. The cell in front (row 6, column D) is lava (L), which is deadly, so moving forward is unsafe. Turning right to face east might allow movement towards more safe cells and towards the goal visible at row 5, column F.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing east at row 7, column D, but the cell ahead (row 7, column E) is a wall (#), so I cannot move forward. Turning left to face north could allow movement onto a floor cell in front at row 6, column D, but that cell is lava as per the last observation. Instead, turning left and then moving forward might be unsafe. Given I cannot move forward or into lava, I will turn left once to face north, reassess, and seek an alternative path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I have turned to face north again. The cell in front of me is lava (L) at row 6, column D, so moving forward is unsafe. I cannot move forward, and turning right or left again would face me east or west. Facing east (right) the cell is floor (.) but previously there was a wall at that position in earlier view, indicating it might now be accessible. To find a safe path to the goal at row 5 column F, I will try turning right again to face east to move towards the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing east again, standing at row 7, column D. The cell in front of me at row 7, column E is a wall (#), so I cannot move forward. Turning right will face me south where there is a wall, and turning left faces me north where there is lava. The only option left is to turn left to face north and try to find an alternate path, as moving forward into lava is unsafe.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing north again, back to my original orientation. The cell in front at row 6, column D is lava, so I cannot move forward. I cannot move forward safely, so I will try turning left to face west and check if I have a better path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>
